today_news.title: OpenAI cofounder envisions "almost no interface" future where nobody learns software anymore
today_news.summary: Greg Brockman admits ChatGPT's plugins, heavily marketed in 2023, failed "because the models weren't ready." Instead of app extensions, he sees the future in an invisible, context-aware agent. But OpenAI's own Codex is still light-years from that vision.

today_news.title: Open-source tool pxpipe hides text in PNGs to cut Claude Code and Fable 5 token costs up to 70%
today_news.summary: The open-source tool pxpipe converts long text prompts for Claude Code into compact PNGs, exploiting the fact that Anthropic charges for images by pixel size, not text content. Developer Steven Chong reports cost savings of 59 to 70 percent, at the price of accuracy and speed.

today_news.title: AI search agents don't fail at searching, they fail at asking the right questions when queries get ambiguous
today_news.summary: AI search agents rarely fail at multi-step research because of the search itself. Their real problem is not asking the user for clarification when queries are ambiguous. A new benchmark called DiscoBench shows that models searching repeatedly instead of asking follow-up questions actually perform wo...

today_news.title: Anthropic developer shares prompting tips for Fable 5 that focus on finding your own blind spots first
today_news.summary: Anthropic developer Thariq Shihipar argues that with Claude's new model, Fable 5, the bottleneck is no longer the model itself but the user's blind spots. He describes techniques like blindspot passes and structured interviews that programmers can use to systematically uncover their unconscious know...

today_news.title: A 26,000-student study shows AI's hidden learning cost takes two full years to surface
today_news.summary: A study of more than 26,000 Chinese students found that AI users finished homework faster and scored higher but performed up to 24 percent worse on exams. The full impact on entrance exam results took about two years to show up, meaning short-term studies systematically underestimate the damage.
